title: 'Harry sued' and Vance warns Iran 'don't try to play us'
description: News of the Duke of Sussex being sued for defamation by a charity he co-founded dominates several of Saturday's front pages.
content: Several front pages focus on the warning from European airports that their jet fuel stocks could run dry within three weeks because of the Iran war, with the Daily Telegraph saying, external "half term holiday flights are at risk". It suggests airlin... [1512 chars]

title: Iran delegation arrives in Pakistan for US talks, as Lebanon and Israel to discuss ceasefire
description: Israel and Lebanon are due to talk on Tuesday in Washington, with discussions mediated by the US, according to the Lebanese president's office.
content:
Israel says it will not discuss ceasefire with Hezbollah as Lebanon talks to begin Tuesday

title: Final push for votes as challenger to Hungary's Orbán scents victory
description: As Péter Magyar's opposition movement leads in the polls, tens of thousands of anti-Orbán supporters fill Heroes' Square in Budapest.
content:
On the eve of Hungary's bitterly fought and highly significant election, the two main rivals are taking their campaigns to the wire, as Péter Magyar attempts to end 16 years of continuous rule by Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party.
"We're at the gates of a ... [6226 chars]

title: Masters 2026: Rory McIlroy tightens grip on Masters with record 36-hole lead at Augusta
description: Rory McIlroy opens up a six-shot lead at the Masters - a record after 36 holes - as he sharpens his focus on securing a rare back-to-back win.
content:
When Rory McIlroy chatted to the great Jack Nicklaus for advice about the best way to defend his Masters title, he was given a succinct piece of advice.
"He told me 'no double bogeys'," laughed McIlroy.
An obvious tip maybe, but it is easier said tha... [5967 chars]

title: Artemis II live: Crew begin final manoeuvres for re-entry to Earth's atmosphere
description: The astronauts are expected to splash down in the Pacific Ocean at 01:07 BST (20:07 EDT) on Saturday.
content:
Orion hits the atmosphere - and blackout begins
Pallab Ghosh
Science correspondent
Orion has hit the entry interface — 400,000 feet above the Earth's surface, southeast of Hawaii — and the blackout has begun.
